stories.headline: Iran Keeps Up Attacks Before US Deadline, Dimming Peace Chances
stories.summary: Iran pressed on with attacks across the Persian Gulf hours before US President Donald Trump’s deadline to agree to a ceasefire plan, clouding prospects for a breakthrough in efforts to end the war.

stories.headline: Markets send mixed signals ahead of Trump's deadline to escalate Iran war
stories.summary: European and Asian markets sent mixed signals on Tuesday in anticipation of US President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to agree to a deal "before all Hell will reign down on them".

stories.headline: India's high-growth economy gets a Middle East oil shock

stories.headline: Ackman Proposes €56 Billion Universal Music Deal
stories.summary: Billionaire activist Bill Ackman has pitched a deal which would see his investment firm, Pershing Square, combine with Universal Music, the world’s largest music company. Bloomberg's Benoit Berthelot breaks down the proposal. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson to Resign Amid Mounting Losses
stories.summary: Air India Chief Executive Officer Campbell Wilson has resigned as the Tata Group-owned carrier confronts record losses and operational setbacks.

stories.headline: UK Private Sector Flatlines in March as Stagflation Fears Rise
stories.summary: Britain’s private sector growth ground to a halt in the first month of the Iran war, according to a survey that pointed to a sudden loss in momentum and the threat of “stagflation.”

stories.headline: Ackman’s Pershing Square Proposes Universal Music Merger
stories.summary: Pershing Square has proposed a combination with Universal Music that would move the listing into a US-based acquisition vehicle. Bill Ackman’s fund said the deal will value the world’s biggest music label at a 78% premium to its last closing price. Bloomberg's Neil Campling reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB’s Elderson Presses Green-Transition Pitch After Energy Surge
stories.summary: Surging energy costs due to the war in the Middle East are the latest reminder for Europe to urgently reduce its reliance on fossil fuels, one of the European Central Bank’s top officials wrote in an op-ed published by the Financial Times.

stories.headline: India Bank Options Hedging Costs Climb on RBI, Geopolitical Risk
stories.summary: The cost of hedging against swings in a key Indian banking gauge relative to the broader market has surged, as traders brace for the central bank’s rate decision as well as a potential escalation in the Middle East war.

stories.headline: What to Watch in the Election to Succeed Marjorie Taylor Greene in Georgia

stories.summary: Indonesian market reforms to meet MSCI Inc.’s demands may stave off a downgrade though they don’t go far enough to prevent a lower weighting in global indexes, some analysts said.

stories.headline: Fact-Checking Republicans’ Misleading Claims About Problematic Elections
stories.summary: President Trump, his administration and G.O.P. lawmakers have claimed widespread issues with mailed ballots and fraudulent voting, but the evidence doesn’t support them.

stories.summary: Gross domestic product per capita in purchasing power standards varies significantly across Europe in 2025. One in three people in the EU lives in a country where GDP per capita in PPS is above the EU average.

stories.headline: Trump warns Iran it could be destroyed 'in one night' as deadline nears
stories.summary: Donald Trump has once again escalated threats against Iran, warning the country could be “taken out in one night” as the US deadline for Tehran approaches. Iran has rejected a proposed ceasefire, insisting on a permanent end to the war, while fighting continues to intensify across the region.

stories.summary: Gold held steady, following a two-day decline, as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s latest threat to destroy Iranian infrastructure and the impact of a protracted war on economic growth.
